forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+project: ai-panel
+title: grep_files (and file tools) must resolve search root from board/session cwd, not process CWD (which is `/`)
+---
+## P0 — root cause of "a simple grep hung forever" in the AI panel
+
+### What happened (evidence, session 01KT9QXP…, pid 40625)
+The qwen agent issued `grep_files {pattern:"builtin.*partial", output_mode:"content", case_insensitive:true}` with **no `path`** at 11:38:53. The handler never returned. Exactly 300s later (11:43:53) the MCP stream tore down (`serve finished quit_reason=Closed`), after which the agent looped on `GET /mcp → 404` indefinitely. The turn never recovered.
+
+### Root cause (two compounding faults)
+`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/grep/mod.rs::execute_grep`:
+1. **Default root = process CWD.** When `request.path` is `None` (around line 176) it falls back to `std::env::current_dir()`. For the **bundled macOS GUI app, the process CWD is `/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`). See memory `gui-cwd-readonly`. So the search root was the **filesystem root**.
+2. **Raw, un-ignored walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no gitignore / hidden-dir / `target` / `.git` filtering and no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, this recursively walks the **entire Mac filesystem**, regex-matching every file. It effectively never finishes.
+
+`send_mcp_log` (tool_registry.rs:1963) is fire-and-forget (`let _ = peer.send_notification(...).await`) and is **not** the blocker — the walk is. The `Failed to forward MCP notification: channel closed` warning is benign (broadcast with no subscribers).
+
+### Fix
+- The ACP session is already created with `cwd = …/.kanban` (log line 11). Tools must derive their search/working root from that **session/board working directory** (per user: the directory *containing* `.kanban`), **never** `std::env::current_dir()`. Thread the work_dir through `ToolContext` and use it as the default root.
+- Replace the raw `WalkDir` with an ignore-aware, bounded walk (e.g. `ignore::WalkBuilder` — respects `.gitignore`, skips hidden/`target`/`.git`), matching ripgrep semantics users expect.
+- Refuse/anchor an unscoped search at `/` (defensive guard).
+- **Audit sibling file tools** (`files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`) for the same `std::env::current_dir()` reliance and fix them the same way — this is cross-cutting (also affects the `sah` CLI when launched from an unexpected CWD).
+
+### Verification
+Real-path test: invoke `grep_files` with `path:None` while process CWD ≠ workspace, assert it searches the board dir and returns promptly; assert it does not descend into `target/`/`.git`.
\ No newline at end of file

----
-## P0 — root cause of "a simple grep hung forever" in the AI panel
-
-### What happened (evidence, session 01KT9QXP…, pid 40625)
-The qwen agent issued `grep_files {pattern:"builtin.*partial", output_mode:"content", case_insensitive:true}` with **no `path`** at 11:38:53. The handler never returned. Exactly 300s later (11:43:53) the MCP stream tore down (`serve finished quit_reason=Closed`), after which the agent looped on `GET /mcp → 404` indefinitely. The turn never recovered.
-
-### Root cause (two compounding faults)
-`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/grep/mod.rs::execute_grep`:
-1. **Default root = process CWD.** When `request.path` is `None` (around line 176) it falls back to `std::env::current_dir()`. For the **bundled macOS GUI app, the process CWD is `/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`). See memory `gui-cwd-readonly`. So the search root was the **filesystem root**.
-2. **Raw, un-ignored walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no gitignore / hidden-dir / `target` / `.git` filtering and no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, this recursively walks the **entire Mac filesystem**, regex-matching every file. It effectively never finishes.
-
-`send_mcp_log` (tool_registry.rs:1963) is fire-and-forget (`let _ = peer.send_notification(...).await`) and is **not** the blocker — the walk is. The `Failed to forward MCP notification: channel closed` warning is benign (broadcast with no subscribers).
-
-### Fix
-- The ACP session is already created with `cwd = …/.kanban` (log line 11). Tools must derive their search/working root from that **session/board working directory** (per user: the directory *containing* `.kanban`), **never** `std::env::current_dir()`. Thread the work_dir through `ToolContext` and use it as the default root.
-- Replace the raw `WalkDir` with an ignore-aware, bounded walk (e.g. `ignore::WalkBuilder` — respects `.gitignore`, skips hidden/`target`/`.git`), matching ripgrep semantics users expect.
-- Refuse/anchor an unscoped search at `/` (defensive guard).
-- **Audit sibling file tools** (`files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`) for the same `std::env::current_dir()` reliance and fix them the same way — this is cross-cutting (also affects the `sah` CLI when launched from an unexpected CWD).
-
-### Verification
-Real-path test: invoke `grep_files` with `path:None` while process CWD ≠ workspace, assert it searches the board dir and returns promptly; assert it does not descend into `target/`/`.git`.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -4,25 +4,34 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '80'
 project: ai-panel
-title: grep_files (and file tools) must resolve search root from board/session cwd, not process CWD (which is `/`)
+title: All tools must resolve CWD from the session working directory (the board dir), never std::env::current_dir(); grep must honor .gitignore
 ---
 ## P0 — root cause of "a simple grep hung forever" in the AI panel

-### What happened (evidence, session 01KT9QXP…, pid 40625)
-The qwen agent issued `grep_files {pattern:"builtin.*partial", output_mode:"content", case_insensitive:true}` with **no `path`** at 11:38:53. The handler never returned. Exactly 300s later (11:43:53) the MCP stream tore down (`serve finished quit_reason=Closed`), after which the agent looped on `GET /mcp → 404` indefinitely. The turn never recovered.
+### Binding requirement (per user, previously specified)
+**The working directory of a board IS the working directory of its agent session.** Every tool MUST resolve its CWD / default root from that **session working directory**, and MUST NOT call `std::env::current_dir()`. The app process CWD is irrelevant (and is `/` for the bundled GUI app). The board is not in `/`; the session is rooted at the board's directory — comply with that.
+
+### What happened (evidence — session 01KT9QXP…, pid 40625)
+The qwen agent issued `grep_files {pattern:"builtin.*partial", output_mode:"content", case_insensitive:true}` with **no `path`** at 11:38:53. The handler never returned. Exactly 300s later (11:43:53) the MCP stream tore down (`serve finished quit_reason=Closed`); the agent then looped on `GET /mcp → 404` indefinitely. The turn never recovered. The "forever" the user saw is this hang.

 ### Root cause (two compounding faults)
 `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/grep/mod.rs::execute_grep`:
-1. **Default root = process CWD.** When `request.path` is `None` (around line 176) it falls back to `std::env::current_dir()`. For the **bundled macOS GUI app, the process CWD is `/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`). See memory `gui-cwd-readonly`. So the search root was the **filesystem root**.
-2. **Raw, un-ignored walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no gitignore / hidden-dir / `target` / `.git` filtering and no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, this recursively walks the **entire Mac filesystem**, regex-matching every file. It effectively never finishes.
+1. **Uses process CWD instead of the session working dir.** With `request.path == None` (~line 176) it falls back to `std::env::current_dir()`. For the bundled GUI app that is **`/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`; memory `gui-cwd-readonly`). So it rooted the search at the **filesystem root** — even though the session/board dir was known.
+2. **Raw, un-ignored, unbounded walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no `.gitignore` honoring, no hidden/`target`/`.git` skip, no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, this walks the entire Mac filesystem, regex-matching every file → effectively never finishes.

-`send_mcp_log` (tool_registry.rs:1963) is fire-and-forget (`let _ = peer.send_notification(...).await`) and is **not** the blocker — the walk is. The `Failed to forward MCP notification: channel closed` warning is benign (broadcast with no subscribers).
+`send_mcp_log` (tool_registry.rs:1963) is fire-and-forget and is NOT the blocker. The `Failed to forward MCP notification: channel closed` warn is benign (broadcast, no subscribers).

-### Fix
-- The ACP session is already created with `cwd = …/.kanban` (log line 11). Tools must derive their search/working root from that **session/board working directory** (per user: the directory *containing* `.kanban`), **never** `std::env::current_dir()`. Thread the work_dir through `ToolContext` and use it as the default root.
-- Replace the raw `WalkDir` with an ignore-aware, bounded walk (e.g. `ignore::WalkBuilder` — respects `.gitignore`, skips hidden/`target`/`.git`), matching ripgrep semantics users expect.
-- Refuse/anchor an unscoped search at `/` (defensive guard).
-- **Audit sibling file tools** (`files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`) for the same `std::env::current_dir()` reliance and fix them the same way — this is cross-cutting (also affects the `sah` CLI when launched from an unexpected CWD).
+### The session working dir is already available — just not consumed
+- The MCP server is constructed **with** a working dir: `McpServer::new_with_work_dir(PromptLibrary, working_dir, …)` — `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/mcp_integration.rs:48`.
+- The in-process board server already knows the board root: log line 4 → `started in-process MCP server for board board=/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer` (the directory **containing** `.kanban`).
+- The gap: this working dir is not threaded into `ToolContext` and tool handlers don't use it.

-### Verification
-Real-path test: invoke `grep_files` with `path:None` while process CWD ≠ workspace, assert it searches the board dir and returns promptly; assert it does not descend into `target/`/`.git`.
\ No newline at end of file
+### Fix (required)
+1. Thread the session/board working directory through `ToolContext` (single source of truth, set from `new_with_work_dir`).
+2. In `execute_grep` (and **every** file tool: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell, etc.), resolve the default root from `context.work_dir` (the session working directory). **Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers** — add a lint/test guard.
+3. grep **must honor `.gitignore`** — replace the raw `WalkDir` with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`, supports bounded depth), matching ripgrep semantics users expect.
+4. Defensive guard: refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`.
+
+### Verification (real-path)
+- With process CWD = `/` and a session work_dir = a repo, call `grep_files` with `path:None`: assert it searches the repo, returns promptly, and does **not** descend into `target/`, `.git/`, or `.gitignore`d paths.
+- Guard test: grep handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
\ No newline at end of file

+1. **Default root = process CWD.** When `request.path` is `None` (around line 176) it falls back to `std::env::current_dir()`. For the **bundled macOS GUI app, the process CWD is `/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`). See memory `gui-cwd-readonly`. So the search root was the **filesystem root**.
+2. **Raw, un-ignored walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no gitignore / hidden-dir / `target` / `.git` filtering and no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, this recursively walks the **entire Mac filesystem**, regex-matching every file. It effectively never finishes.

-### The session working dir is already available — just not consumed
-- The MCP server is constructed **with** a working dir: `McpServer::new_with_work_dir(PromptLibrary, working_dir, …)` — `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/mcp_integration.rs:48`.
-- The in-process board server already knows the board root: log line 4 → `started in-process MCP server for board board=/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer` (the directory **containing** `.kanban`).
-- The gap: this working dir is not threaded into `ToolContext` and tool handlers don't use it.
+`send_mcp_log` (tool_registry.rs:1963) is fire-and-forget (`let _ = peer.send_notification(...).await`) and is **not** the blocker — the walk is. The `Failed to forward MCP notification: channel closed` warning is benign (broadcast with no subscribers).

-1. Thread the session/board working directory through `ToolContext` (single source of truth, set from `new_with_work_dir`).
-2. In `execute_grep` (and **every** file tool: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell, etc.), resolve the default root from `context.work_dir` (the session working directory). **Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers** — add a lint/test guard.
-3. grep **must honor `.gitignore`** — replace the raw `WalkDir` with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`, supports bounded depth), matching ripgrep semantics users expect.

Tools must derive their search/working root from that **session/board working directory** (per user: the directory *containing* `.kanban`), **never** `std::env::current_dir()`. Thread the work_dir through `ToolContext` and use it as the default root.
+- Replace the raw `WalkDir` with an ignore-aware, bounded walk (e.g. `ignore::WalkBuilder` — respects `.gitignore`, skips hidden/`target`/`.git`), matching ripgrep semantics users expect.
+- Refuse/anchor an unscoped search at `/` (defensive guard).
+- **Audit sibling file tools** (`files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`) for the same `std::env::current_dir()` reliance and fix them the same way — this is cross-cutting (also affects the `sah` CLI when launched from an unexpected CWD).

-### Verification (real-path)
-- With process CWD = `/` and a session work_dir = a repo, call `grep_files` with `path:None`: assert it searches the repo, returns promptly, and does **not** descend into `target/`, `.git/`, or `.gitignore`d paths.
-- Guard test: grep handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.

Exactly 300s later (11:43:53) the MCP stream tore down (`serve finished quit_reason=Closed`); the agent then looped on `GET /mcp → 404` forever. That hang is the "forever" the user saw.

 ### Root cause (two compounding faults)
 `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/grep/mod.rs::execute_grep`:
-1. **Uses process CWD instead of the session working dir.** With `request.path == None` (~line 176) it falls back to `std::env::current_dir()`. For the bundled GUI app that is **`/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`; memory `gui-cwd-readonly`). So it rooted the search at the **filesystem root** — even though the session/board dir was known.
-2. **Raw, un-ignored, unbounded walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no `.gitignore` honoring, no hidden/`target`/`.git` skip, no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, this walks the entire Mac filesystem, regex-matching every file → effectively never finishes.

+### The session working dir is already known — just not applied
 - The MCP server is constructed **with** a working dir: `McpServer::new_with_work_dir(PromptLibrary, working_dir, …)` — `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/mcp_integration.rs:48`.
-- The in-process board server already knows the board root: log line 4 → `started in-process MCP server for board board=/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer` (the directory **containing** `.kanban`).
-- The gap: this working dir is not threaded into `ToolContext` and tool handlers don't use it.
+- The in-process board server already knows the board root: log line 4 → `started in-process MCP server for board board=/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer` (the dir **containing** `.kanban`).
+- Gap: that working dir is neither threaded into `ToolContext` nor used to set the server's CWD per session.

-### Fix (required)
-1. Thread the session/board working directory through `ToolContext` (single source of truth, set from `new_with_work_dir`).
-2. In `execute_grep` (and **every** file tool: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell, etc.), resolve the default root from `context.work_dir` (the session working directory). **Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers** — add a lint/test guard.
-3. grep **must honor `.gitignore`** — replace the raw `WalkDir` with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`, supports bounded depth), matching ripgrep semantics users expect.
-4. Defensive guard: refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`.
+### Two viable approaches (do BOTH where each applies)
+**A. Thread session work_dir through `ToolContext` (required for the in-process multi-board server).** Tool handlers resolve their default root from `context.work_dir`, never `std::env::current_dir()`. This is the ONLY correct option for the shared in-process server, because **process CWD is global** and one app process hosts multiple boards/sessions — you cannot set a per-session CWD there.
+
+**B. Start the MCP tool server with the session's CWD set (correct for per-process servers).** For servers that run one-process-per-session — `kanban-cli serve`, `sah` CLI — `std::chdir` to the board dir at startup so even `current_dir()` resolves correctly. Clean, but does NOT generalize to the in-process multi-board host (approach A still needed there).
+
+### Required regardless of approach
+- **Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers** — add a lint/test guard; resolve root from the session working dir.
+- grep **must honor `.gitignore`** — replace raw `WalkDir` with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`, supports bounded depth), matching ripgrep semantics.
+- Apply the same root-resolution fix to **all** file tools: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell.
+- Defensive guard: refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`.

 ### Verification (real-path)
-- With process CWD = `/` and a session work_dir = a repo, call `grep_files` with `path:None`: assert it searches the repo, returns promptly, and does **not** descend into `target/`, `.git/`, or `.gitignore`d paths.
-- Guard test: grep handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Process CWD = `/`, session work_dir = a repo: `grep_files` with `path:None` searches the repo, returns promptly, and skips `target/`, `.git/`, and `.gitignore`d paths.
+- Guard test: tool handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
\ No newline at end of file

For the bundled GUI app that is **`/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`; memory `gui-cwd-readonly`). So it rooted the search at the filesystem root even though the board dir was known.
-2. **Raw, un-ignored, unbounded walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no `.gitignore` honoring, no hidden/`target`/`.git` skip, no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, it walks the entire Mac filesystem, regex-matching every file → effectively never finishes.
+1. **Uses process CWD instead of the session working dir.** With `request.path == None` (~line 176) it falls back to `std::env::current_dir()`. For the bundled GUI app that is **`/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`; memory `gui-cwd-readonly`). So it rooted the search at the **filesystem root** — even though the session/board dir was known.
+2. **Raw, un-ignored, unbounded walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no `.gitignore` honoring, no hidden/`target`/`.git` skip, no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, this walks the entire Mac filesystem, regex-matching every file → effectively never finishes.

 `send_mcp_log` (tool_registry.rs:1963) is fire-and-forget and is NOT the blocker. The `Failed to forward MCP notification: channel closed` warn is benign (broadcast, no subscribers).

-### The session working dir is already known — just not applied
+### The session working dir is already available — just not consumed
 - The MCP server is constructed **with** a working dir: `McpServer::new_with_work_dir(PromptLibrary, working_dir, …)` — `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/mcp_integration.rs:48`.
-- The in-process board server already knows the board root: log line 4 → `started in-process MCP server for board board=/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer` (the dir **containing** `.kanban`).
-- Gap: that working dir is neither threaded into `ToolContext` nor used to set the server's CWD per session.
-
-### Two viable approaches (do BOTH where each applies)
-**A. Thread session work_dir through `ToolContext` (required for the in-process multi-board server).** Tool handlers resolve their default root from `context.work_dir`, never `std::env::current_dir()`. This is the ONLY correct option for the shared in-process server, because **process CWD is global** and one app process hosts multiple boards/sessions — you cannot set a per-session CWD there.
-
-**B. Start the MCP tool server with the session's CWD set (correct for per-process servers).** For servers that run one-process-per-session — `kanban-cli serve`, `sah` CLI — `std::chdir` to the board dir at startup so even `current_dir()` resolves correctly. Clean, but does NOT generalize to the in-process multi-board host (approach A still needed there).
+- The in-process board server already knows the board root: log line 4 → `started in-process MCP server for board board=/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer` (the directory **containing** `.kanban`).
+- The gap: this working dir is not threaded into `ToolContext` and tool handlers don't use it.

-### Required regardless of approach
-- **Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers** — add a lint/test guard; resolve root from the session working dir.
-- grep **must honor `.gitignore`** — replace raw `WalkDir` with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`, supports bounded depth), matching ripgrep semantics.
-- Apply the same root-resolution fix to **all** file tools: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell.
-- Defensive guard: refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`.
+### Fix (required)
+1. Thread the session/board working directory through `ToolContext` (single source of truth, set from `new_with_work_dir`).
+2. In `execute_grep` (and **every** file tool: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell, etc.), resolve the default root from `context.work_dir` (the session working directory). **Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers** — add a lint/test guard.
+3. grep **must honor `.gitignore`** — replace the raw `WalkDir` with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`, supports bounded depth), matching ripgrep semantics users expect.
+4. Defensive guard: refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`.

 ### Verification (real-path)
-- Process CWD = `/`, session work_dir = a repo: `grep_files` with `path:None` searches the repo, returns promptly, and skips `target/`, `.git/`, and `.gitignore`d paths.
-- Guard test: tool handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
\ No newline at end of file
+- With process CWD = `/` and a session work_dir = a repo, call `grep_files` with `path:None`: assert it searches the repo, returns promptly, and does **not** descend into `target/`, `.git/`, or `.gitignore`d paths.
+- Guard test: grep handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -39,4 +39,16 @@

 ### Verification (real-path)
 - Process CWD = `/`, session work_dir = a repo: `grep_files` with `path:None` searches the repo, returns promptly, and skips `target/`, `.git/`, and `.gitignore`d paths.
-- Guard test: tool handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Guard test: tool handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-04 18:35)
+
+Approach A (thread `work_dir` through `ToolContext`) is implemented and correctly wired: `server.rs:656` sets `tool_context.working_dir`, and all six file/shell handlers (grep, glob, read, write, edit, shell) resolve their root via `ToolContext::session_root()`. grep now uses `ignore::WalkBuilder` with gitignore/hidden honored. New integration tests `session_root_resolution.rs` (real-path reproduction + static `current_dir` guard) pass; file/shell unit suites green (174 + 173). The change aligns with `ARCHITECTURE.md` "Process-global mutable state" guidance and uses `CurrentDirGuard` + `serial_test(cwd)` for isolation. No blockers.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/glob/mod.rs:103-119` — glob has no equivalent of grep's filesystem-root defensive guard (`grep/mod.rs:187`). The task required "refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`" applied to all file tools. An unscoped glob whose `session_root()` resolves to `/` will walk from the filesystem root; it is bounded by `MAX_RESULTS` (10_000) and gitignore so it won't hang the way raw-WalkDir grep did, but it can still be very slow and stream tens of thousands of paths. Add the same `search_dir.parent().is_none()` refusal (or factor a shared `reject_filesystem_root(&search_dir)` helper used by both grep and glob).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tool_registry.rs:590-595` — `session_root()` falls back to `current_dir()` then to `PathBuf::from(".")`. The `.` fallback silently defeats grep's `/`-guard: `Path::new(".").parent()` is `Some("")`, not `None`, so the guard does not fire, and the walk then proceeds rooted at a relative `.` whose meaning is the very process CWD this change exists to avoid. In the in-process host the constructor always threads `work_dir`, so this is defense-in-depth, but consider making the no-work-dir, no-current-dir case an explicit error in the handlers rather than walking `.`.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/read/mod.rs:208-212` — the path is validated twice: `validator.validate_path(&request.path)` produces an absolute `validated_path`, which is then passed as a string into `SecureFileAccess::default_secure(session_root).read(...)`, which validates again. Harmless (idempotent) but redundant; pass the already-validated path or drop the first validation.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/grep/mod.rs:187-196` — the filesystem-root guard message ("Refusing to search the filesystem root: …") prints `search_dir.display()`, which is fine for `/` but would read oddly if `search_dir` were ever a relative `.` (see the `session_root` nit). Minor.
\ No newline at end of file

 ## P0 — root cause of "a simple grep hung forever" in the AI panel

-### Binding requirement (per user, previously specified)
-**The working directory of a board IS the working directory of its agent session.** Every tool MUST operate rooted at that **session working directory**. The app process CWD is irrelevant (and is `/` for the bundled GUI app). The board is not in `/` — comply with the board-dir-as-session-CWD contract.
+(Original description and prior review findings retained below.)

-### What happened (evidence — session 01KT9QXP…, pid 40625)
-The qwen agent issued `grep_files {pattern:"builtin.*partial", output_mode:"content", case_insensitive:true}` with **no `path`** at 11:38:53. The handler never returned. Exactly 300s later (11:43:53) the MCP stream tore down (`serve finished quit_reason=Closed`); the agent then looped on `GET /mcp → 404` forever. That hang is the "forever" the user saw.
+## Resolution of 2026-06-04 18:35 review findings (claude-code)

-### Root cause (two compounding faults)
-`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/grep/mod.rs::execute_grep`:
-1. **Uses process CWD instead of the session working dir.** With `request.path == None` (~line 176) it falls back to `std::env::current_dir()`. For the bundled GUI app that is **`/`** (confirmed live: `lsof -p 40625 -d cwd` → `/`; memory `gui-cwd-readonly`). So it rooted the search at the filesystem root even though the board dir was known.
-2. **Raw, un-ignored, unbounded walker.** Lines ~217-252 use a plain `WalkDir::new(search_dir)` with **no `.gitignore` honoring, no hidden/`target`/`.git` skip, no depth bound**. Rooted at `/`, it walks the entire Mac filesystem, regex-matching every file → effectively never finishes.
+All four findings addressed.
+
+- [x] **Warning — glob filesystem-root guard.** Factored a shared `reject_filesystem_root(&Path)` helper into `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/shared_utils.rs` and applied it in both grep (`grep/mod.rs`) and glob (`glob/mod.rs`) right after the search dir is resolved.
+- [x] **Warning — `session_root()` `.` fallback defeats the guard.** The shared helper also rejects any non-absolute root (bare relative `.`/empty), so the last-resort fallback can never silently root a walk at the process CWD (`Path::new(".").parent()` is `Some("")`). Updated the `session_root()` docstring in `tool_registry.rs` to state the guard catches this.
+- [x] **Nit — read double-validation.** Removed the first `FilePathValidator::validate_path` in `read/mod.rs`; the request path now flows straight into `SecureFileAccess::read`, which validates once. Removed the now-unused import.
+- [x] **Nit — guard message wording.** The shared helper emits a distinct, accurate message for the filesystem-root case vs. the unresolved-relative case.

-`send_mcp_log` (tool_registry.rs:1963) is fire-and-forget and is NOT the blocker. The `Failed to forward MCP notification: channel closed` warn is benign (broadcast, no subscribers).
+### Tests
+- 4 new unit tests for `reject_filesystem_root` (root / `.` / empty rejected, normal dir accepted).
+- 2 new integration tests in `session_root_resolution.rs`: `grep_unscoped_refuses_filesystem_root`, `glob_unscoped_refuses_filesystem_root`.
+- file unit suite 178 passed, shell 173 passed, integration session_root_resolution 5 passed, `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-tools --lib --tests` clean (zero warnings).

-### The session working dir is already known — just not applied
-- The MCP server is constructed **with** a working dir: `McpServer::new_with_work_dir(PromptLibrary, working_dir, …)` — `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/mcp_integration.rs:48`.
-- The in-process board server already knows the board root: log line 4 → `started in-process MCP server for board board=/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer` (the dir **containing** `.kanban`).
-- Gap: that working dir is neither threaded into `ToolContext` nor used to set the server's CWD per session.
+---

-### Two viable approaches (do BOTH where each applies)
-**A. Thread session work_dir through `ToolContext` (required for the in-process multi-board server).** Tool handlers resolve their default root from `context.work_dir`, never `std::env::current_dir()`. This is the ONLY correct option for the shared in-process server, because **process CWD is global** and one app process hosts multiple boards/sessions — you cannot set a per-session CWD there.
+### Original task

-**B. Start the MCP tool server with the session's CWD set (correct for per-process servers).** For servers that run one-process-per-session — `kanban-cli serve`, `sah` CLI — `std::chdir` to the board dir at startup so even `current_dir()` resolves correctly. Clean, but does NOT generalize to the in-process multi-board host (approach A still needed there).
+### Binding requirement (per user, previously specified)
+**The working directory of a board IS the working directory of its agent session.** Every tool MUST operate rooted at that **session working directory**. The app process CWD is irrelevant (and is `/` for the bundled GUI app).

 ### Required regardless of approach
-- **Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers** — add a lint/test guard; resolve root from the session working dir.
-- grep **must honor `.gitignore`** — replace raw `WalkDir` with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`, supports bounded depth), matching ripgrep semantics.
-- Apply the same root-resolution fix to **all** file tools: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell.
+- Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers — lint/test guard; resolve root from the session working dir.
+- grep must honor `.gitignore` — `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`).
+- Apply the same root-resolution fix to all file tools: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell.
 - Defensive guard: refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`.

-### Verification (real-path)
-- Process CWD = `/`, session work_dir = a repo: `grep_files` with `path:None` searches the repo, returns promptly, and skips `target/`, `.git/`, and `.gitignore`d paths.
-- Guard test: tool handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
-
 ## Review Findings (2026-06-04 18:35)

-Approach A (thread `work_dir` through `ToolContext`) is implemented and correctly wired: `server.rs:656` sets `tool_context.working_dir`, and all six file/shell handlers (grep, glob, read, write, edit, shell) resolve their root via `ToolContext::session_root()`. grep now uses `ignore::WalkBuilder` with gitignore/hidden honored. New integration tests `session_root_resolution.rs` (real-path reproduction + static `current_dir` guard) pass; file/shell unit suites green (174 + 173). The change aligns with `ARCHITECTURE.md` "Process-global mutable state" guidance and uses `CurrentDirGuard` + `serial_test(cwd)` for isolation. No blockers.
-
-### Warnings
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/glob/mod.rs:103-119` — glob has no equivalent of grep's filesystem-root defensive guard (`grep/mod.rs:187`). The task required "refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`" applied to all file tools. An unscoped glob whose `session_root()` resolves to `/` will walk from the filesystem root; it is bounded by `MAX_RESULTS` (10_000) and gitignore so it won't hang the way raw-WalkDir grep did, but it can still be very slow and stream tens of thousands of paths. Add the same `search_dir.parent().is_none()` refusal (or factor a shared `reject_filesystem_root(&search_dir)` helper used by both grep and glob).
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tool_registry.rs:590-595` — `session_root()` falls back to `current_dir()` then to `PathBuf::from(".")`. The `.` fallback silently defeats grep's `/`-guard: `Path::new(".").parent()` is `Some("")`, not `None`, so the guard does not fire, and the walk then proceeds rooted at a relative `.` whose meaning is the very process CWD this change exists to avoid. In the in-process host the constructor always threads `work_dir`, so this is defense-in-depth, but consider making the no-work-dir, no-current-dir case an explicit error in the handlers rather than walking `.`.
-
-### Nits
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/read/mod.rs:208-212` — the path is validated twice: `validator.validate_path(&request.path)` produces an absolute `validated_path`, which is then passed as a string into `SecureFileAccess::default_secure(session_root).read(...)`, which validates again. Harmless (idempotent) but redundant; pass the already-validated path or drop the first validation.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/grep/mod.rs:187-196` — the filesystem-root guard message ("Refusing to search the filesystem root: …") prints `search_dir.display()`, which is fine for `/` but would read oddly if `search_dir` were ever a relative `.` (see the `session_root` nit). Minor.
\ No newline at end of file
+Approach A (thread `work_dir` through `ToolContext`) implemented and wired: `server.rs:656` sets `tool_context.working_dir`; all six file/shell handlers resolve root via `ToolContext::session_root()`. grep uses `ignore::WalkBuilder`. No blockers. Warnings + nits above — now all resolved.
\ No newline at end of file

-- 4 new unit tests for `reject_filesystem_root` (root / `.` / empty rejected, normal dir accepted).
-- 2 new integration tests in `session_root_resolution.rs`: `grep_unscoped_refuses_filesystem_root`, `glob_unscoped_refuses_filesystem_root`.
-- file unit suite 178 passed, shell 173 passed, integration session_root_resolution 5 passed, `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-tools --lib --tests` clean (zero warnings).
-
----
+### The session working dir is already known — just not applied
+- The MCP server is constructed **with** a working dir: `McpServer::new_with_work_dir(PromptLibrary, working_dir, …)` — `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/mcp_integration.rs:48`.
+- The in-process board server already knows the board root: log line 4 → `started in-process MCP server for board board=/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer` (the dir **containing** `.kanban`).
+- Gap: that working dir is neither threaded into `ToolContext` nor used to set the server's CWD per session.

-### Original task
+### Two viable approaches (do BOTH where each applies)
+**A. Thread session work_dir through `ToolContext` (required for the in-process multi-board server).** Tool handlers resolve their default root from `context.work_dir`, never `std::env::current_dir()`. This is the ONLY correct option for the shared in-process server, because **process CWD is global** and one app process hosts multiple boards/sessions — you cannot set a per-session CWD there.

-### Binding requirement (per user, previously specified)
-**The working directory of a board IS the working directory of its agent session.** Every tool MUST operate rooted at that **session working directory**. The app process CWD is irrelevant (and is `/` for the bundled GUI app).
+**B. Start the MCP tool server with the session's CWD set (correct for per-process servers).** For servers that run one-process-per-session — `kanban-cli serve`, `sah` CLI — `std::chdir` to the board dir at startup so even `current_dir()` resolves correctly. Clean, but does NOT generalize to the in-process multi-board host (approach A still needed there).

 ### Required regardless of approach
-- Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers — lint/test guard; resolve root from the session working dir.
-- grep must honor `.gitignore` — `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`).
-- Apply the same root-resolution fix to all file tools: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell.
+- **Ban `std::env::current_dir()` in tool handlers** — add a lint/test guard; resolve root from the session working dir.
+- grep **must honor `.gitignore`** — replace raw `WalkDir` with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (respects `.gitignore`/`.ignore`, skips hidden + `target`/`.git`, supports bounded depth), matching ripgrep semantics.
+- Apply the same root-resolution fix to **all** file tools: `files`, `glob_files`, `read_file`, shell.
 - Defensive guard: refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`.

+### Verification (real-path)
+- Process CWD = `/`, session work_dir = a repo: `grep_files` with `path:None` searches the repo, returns promptly, and skips `target/`, `.git/`, and `.gitignore`d paths.
+- Guard test: tool handlers contain no `std::env::current_dir()`.
+
 ## Review Findings (2026-06-04 18:35)

-Approach A (thread `work_dir` through `ToolContext`) implemented and wired: `server.rs:656` sets `tool_context.working_dir`; all six file/shell handlers resolve root via `ToolContext::session_root()`. grep uses `ignore::WalkBuilder`. No blockers. Warnings + nits above — now all resolved.
\ No newline at end of file
+Approach A (thread `work_dir` through `ToolContext`) is implemented and correctly wired: `server.rs:656` sets `tool_context.working_dir`, and all six file/shell handlers (grep, glob, read, write, edit, shell) resolve their root via `ToolContext::session_root()`. grep now uses `ignore::WalkBuilder` with gitignore/hidden honored. New integration tests `session_root_resolution.rs` (real-path reproduction + static `current_dir` guard) pass; file/shell unit suites green (174 + 173). The change aligns with `ARCHITECTURE.md` "Process-global mutable state" guidance and uses `CurrentDirGuard` + `serial_test(cwd)` for isolation. No blockers.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/glob/mod.rs:103-119` — glob has no equivalent of grep's filesystem-root defensive guard (`grep/mod.rs:187`). The task required "refuse / hard-anchor any unscoped search that would resolve to `/`" applied to all file tools. An unscoped glob whose `session_root()` resolves to `/` will walk from the filesystem root; it is bounded by `MAX_RESULTS` (10_000) and gitignore so it won't hang the way raw-WalkDir grep did, but it can still be very slow and stream tens of thousands of paths. Add the same `search_dir.parent().is_none()` refusal (or factor a shared `reject_filesystem_root(&search_dir)` helper used by both grep and glob).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tool_registry.rs:590-595` — `session_root()` falls back to `current_dir()` then to `PathBuf::from(".")`. The `.` fallback silently defeats grep's `/`-guard: `Path::new(".").parent()` is `Some("")`, not `None`, so the guard does not fire, and the walk then proceeds rooted at a relative `.` whose meaning is the very process CWD this change exists to avoid. In the in-process host the constructor always threads `work_dir`, so this is defense-in-depth, but consider making the no-work-dir, no-current-dir case an explicit error in the handlers rather than walking `.`.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/read/mod.rs:208-212` — the path is validated twice: `validator.validate_path(&request.path)` produces an absolute `validated_path`, which is then passed as a string into `SecureFileAccess::default_secure(session_root).read(...)`, which validates again. Harmless (idempotent) but redundant; pass the already-validated path or drop the first validation.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/grep/mod.rs:187-196` — the filesystem-root guard message ("Refusing to search the filesystem root: …") prints `search_dir.display()`, which is fine for `/` but would read oddly if `search_dir` were ever a relative `.` (see the `session_root` nit). Minor.
\ No newline at end of file
